sim-t1546-008-atomic-accessibility-featu.discriminators_tuning: Almost no benign cause — IFEO Debugger on accessibility binaries is a near-certain backdoor.
sim-t1546-008-atomic-accessibility-featu.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 13 (RegistryValueSet on `Image File Execution Options\<binary>\Debugger`), EID 11/2 (replacement of the accessibility binary), EDR file-integrity telemetry.
sim-t1546-008-atomic-accessibility-featu.title: Atomic event: Accessibility feature persistence

sim-t1547-009-atomic-shortcut-modificati.adversary_activity: A `.lnk` shortcut is created or modified — often in the Startup folder, or a common shortcut repointed — so a malicious target runs when the user clicks or at logon. The fixture emits a Sysmon `11 FileCreate`.
sim-t1547-009-atomic-shortcut-modificati.detection_logic: Alert on `.lnk` files created/modified in `\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\` or `\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\`, and on shortcuts whose target is `cmd`/`powershell`/`wscript`/a user-writable binary. LNK target parsing (where available) reveals the payload.
sim-t1547-009-atomic-shortcut-modificati.discriminators_tuning: Installers create Startup shortcuts to signed binaries — alert on script-host/LOLBin targets and user-writable target paths.
sim-t1547-009-atomic-shortcut-modificati.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 11 (FileCreate of `.lnk`, `TargetFilename`), EID 2 (file creation time changed), EDR file telemetry, Startup-folder monitoring.
sim-t1547-009-atomic-shortcut-modificati.title: Atomic event: Shortcut modification persistence

sim-t1547-run-key-persistence.adversary_activity: Writing a value under a Run/RunOnce key launches a payload at user logon or boot (`HKCU\...\Run`, `HKLM\...\Run`, `RunOnce`). The fixture records registry-set-shaped telemetry with the Run key path, without writing the registry.
sim-t1547-run-key-persistence.detection_logic: Alert on `RegistryValueSet` to `\CurrentVersion\Run`, `\RunOnce`, `\Policies\Explorer\Run`, `\Winlogon\Shell`/`Userinit` where `Details` references a script host, a path in `\Temp\`/`\AppData\`/`\Users\Public\`, an encoded command, or a freshly-dropped binary.
sim-t1547-run-key-persistence.discriminators_tuning: Legitimate apps add Run keys at install — this key is high-volume. Weight on the value's target path and content; pointing at user-writable/temp locations or script hosts is the signal.
sim-t1547-run-key-persistence.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 13 (RegistryValueSet: `TargetObject`, `Details`), EDR autostart telemetry, Autoruns baselining.
sim-t1547-run-key-persistence.title: Registry Run key persistence

sim-t1550-002-atomic-pass-the-hash-logon.adversary_activity: Pass-the-Hash authenticates with a stolen NTLM hash instead of a password, enabling lateral movement without cracking credentials. It surfaces as an NTLM network logon. The fixture emits a Security `4624 SuccessfulLogon`.
sim-t1550-002-atomic-pass-the-hash-logon.detection_logic: Alert on `4624` Type 3 NTLM logons for accounts/hosts that normally authenticate via Kerberos, on the PtH signature (`LogonType 3` + `NTLM` + `LogonProcessName=NtLmSsp` + `KeyLength=0`), and on the same account performing NTLM network logons across many hosts in a short window.
sim-t1550-002-atomic-pass-the-hash-logon.discriminators_tuning: Legacy apps and non-domain access legitimately use NTLM — baseline which accounts/hosts use NTLM and alert on Kerberos-normal accounts suddenly using NTLM across multiple targets.
sim-t1550-002-atomic-pass-the-hash-logon.production_log_sources: Security `4624` LogonType **3** with `AuthenticationPackageName=NTLM` and specific `LogonProcessName`/key-length characteristics, `4776` (NTLM credential validation), `4672` for the session.
sim-t1550-002-atomic-pass-the-hash-logon.title: Atomic event: Pass-the-Hash style network logon

sim-t1552-002-atomic-credentials-in-regi.adversary_activity: Querying the registry for stored credentials — autologon `DefaultPassword`, VNC/PuTTY/SNMP secrets, saved RDP/credential-manager material. The fixture emits a Sysmon `1 ProcessCreate`.
sim-t1552-002-atomic-credentials-in-regi.detection_logic: Alert on `reg query` against known credential-bearing keys — `...\Winlogon\DefaultPassword`, `...\RealVNC\`, `...\SimonTatham\PuTTY\Sessions`, `...\SNMP\`, `...\TermServices` — and on broad recursive `reg query /s` searching for `password`.
sim-t1552-002-atomic-credentials-in-regi.discriminators_tuning: Admin scripts query the registry — scope to credential-specific value paths and to `findstr password`-style searches.
sim-t1552-002-atomic-credentials-in-regi.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 1 (`reg.exe query` command lines), EID 13 reads where audited, Security `4688`, `4663` on credential registry hives.
sim-t1552-002-atomic-credentials-in-regi.title: Atomic event: Credentials read from registry

sim-t1552-006-atomic-cloud-credential-fi.adversary_activity: Reading credential files for cloud/IaC tooling or Group Policy Preferences yields reusable secrets — `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.azure/`, `~/.config/gcloud/`, `.kube/config`, and SYSVOL `Groups.xml` (GPP `cpassword`). The fixture emits a structured EDR `FILE_READ` event.
sim-t1552-006-atomic-cloud-credential-fi.detection_logic: Alert on reads of `*/.aws/credentials`, `*/.azure/*`, `*/.config/gcloud/*`, `*/.kube/config`, `*/.ssh/id_*`, and SYSVOL `*\Groups.xml` by an unexpected process or user. GPP `cpassword` retrieval (`Get-GPPPassword`) is a classic AD credential path.
sim-t1552-006-atomic-cloud-credential-fi.discriminators_tuning: The owning CLI tools (`aws`, `az`, `gcloud`, `kubectl`) read these legitimately — alert on access by *other* processes/users, and on SYSVOL `Groups.xml` reads by non-DC hosts.
sim-t1552-006-atomic-cloud-credential-fi.production_log_sources: EDR file-read telemetry (`SensitiveFileAccess`), Sysmon EID 11/`auditd` file watches on those paths, Security `4663` (with SACLs), SYSVOL access logs for `Groups.xml`.
sim-t1552-006-atomic-cloud-credential-fi.title: Atomic event: Cloud credential file access

sim-t1552-web-secret-exposure-canary.adversary_activity: Attackers request well-known sensitive paths hoping for misconfigured exposure of secrets — `/.env`, `/.git/config`, `/config.php.bak`, `/id_rsa`, `/.aws/credentials`, backup archives. The simulation requests these fixed paths; the server returns benign/404 responses and never serves secrets.
sim-t1552-web-secret-exposure-canary.detection_logic: Alert on requests to secret/config/key/backup paths (`.env`, `.git/`, `.svn/`, `*.bak`, `*.old`, `id_rsa`, `*.pem`, `*.sql`, `wp-config.php~`), and treat any **`200`** to such a path as critical — it means a real secret may be exposed. A burst of such 404s indicates targeted hunting.
sim-t1552-web-secret-exposure-canary.discriminators_tuning: Scanners generate these as 404s — the severity pivot is the response code; 404s are recon, 200s are exposure.
sim-t1552-web-secret-exposure-canary.production_log_sources: Web access logs (`uri`, `status`), WAF logs, structured web JSON.
sim-t1552-web-secret-exposure-canary.title: Web-exposed secret and backup file canary

sim-t1555-003-atomic-browser-login-data-.adversary_activity: Infostealers read the browser's saved-password store (`Login Data` SQLite + `Local State` DPAPI key for Chrome/Edge, `logins.json`/`key4.db` for Firefox) to harvest credentials. The fixture emits a Sysmon `11 FileCreate`.
sim-t1555-003-atomic-browser-login-data-.detection_logic: Alert when a **non-browser process** opens/copies `...\User Data\*\Login Data`, `Local State`, `Cookies`, or Firefox `logins.json`/`key4.db`. The decisive signal is a process other than the browser itself touching these files.
sim-t1555-003-atomic-browser-login-data-.discriminators_tuning: Backup/sync tools and the browser's own processes access these — allowlist the browser executables and known sync agents; everything else reading the password DB is high-value.
sim-t1555-003-atomic-browser-login-data-.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 11 (FileCreate/copy of `Login Data`), EID 1 (process accessing the path), EID 10 (access to the browser process for in-memory key), EDR file telemetry.
sim-t1555-003-atomic-browser-login-data-.title: Atomic event: Browser credential store access

sim-t1556-002-atomic-password-filter-dll.adversary_activity: Registering a custom DLL in the LSA `Notification Packages` list installs a password filter that receives every password change in plaintext — a stealthy credential-harvesting persistence. The fixture emits a Sysmon `13 RegistryValueSet`.
sim-t1556-002-atomic-password-filter-dll.detection_logic: Alert on any modification of `Lsa\Notification Packages` adding a non-default DLL, and on a non-Microsoft DLL loaded by `lsass.exe`. Default entries are `rassfm`, `scecli`, etc. — additions are suspect.
sim-t1556-002-atomic-password-filter-dll.discriminators_tuning: A few enterprise password-policy products add legitimate filters — allowlist those specific signed DLLs; everything else is high-severity.
sim-t1556-002-atomic-password-filter-dll.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 13 (RegistryValueSet on `HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\Notification Packages`), EID 7 (the DLL loaded into `lsass.exe`), Security `4614` (notification package loaded).
sim-t1556-002-atomic-password-filter-dll.title: Atomic event: Password filter DLL registration

sim-t1560-001-atomic-archive-with-rar.adversary_activity: Staged data is compressed (often password-protected/split) before exfiltration — `rar a -p`, `7z a -p`, `Compress-Archive`, `tar czf`. The fixture emits a Sysmon `1 ProcessCreate`.
sim-t1560-001-atomic-archive-with-rar.detection_logic: Alert on archiving utilities with **password flags** (`-p`, `-hp`), on archives created in staging/temp directories, on `rar`/`7z` present where it isn't normally installed, and on large archives created shortly before an outbound transfer. Password-protected archives of collected data are a strong pre-exfil signal.
sim-t1560-001-atomic-archive-with-rar.discriminators_tuning: Backup and legitimate compression are common — weight on password protection, staging locations, unusual tools, and proximity to exfil/network activity.
sim-t1560-001-atomic-archive-with-rar.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 1 (`rar.exe`/`7z.exe`/`Compress-Archive`/`tar`, `CommandLine`), EID 11 (the archive file created), Security `4688`.
sim-t1560-001-atomic-archive-with-rar.title: Atomic event: Archive collected data with utility

sim-t1562-001-atomic-disable-defender-re.adversary_activity: Disabling Microsoft Defender (real-time protection, AMSI, cloud submission) removes a primary defensive control (`Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true`, registry `DisableAntiSpyware`, `Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath`). The fixture emits a Defender `5007 ConfigurationChanged`.
sim-t1562-001-atomic-disable-defender-re.detection_logic: Alert on `5001`/`5007` indicating real-time protection or tamper-protection disabled, on `Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath`/`-ExclusionExtension`/`-ExclusionProcess` (attackers carve out exclusions), and on registry writes disabling Defender. Exclusion additions are a frequently-missed, high-value signal.
sim-t1562-001-atomic-disable-defender-re.discriminators_tuning: IT and AV-migration legitimately change Defender config — correlate with change tickets; exclusion additions and tamper-protection-off events warrant scrutiny regardless.
sim-t1562-001-atomic-disable-defender-re.production_log_sources: Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational: `5007` (config changed), `5001` (real-time protection disabled), `5010`/`5012` (scanning disabled); Sysmon EID 1 (`Set-MpPreference`); registry EID 13 on Defender keys.
sim-t1562-001-atomic-disable-defender-re.title: Atomic event: Impair defenses by disabling Defender

sim-t1562-004-atomic-disable-windows-fir.adversary_activity: Disabling the host firewall removes egress/ingress controls to enable C2 or lateral movement (`netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off`, `Set-NetFirewallProfile -Enabled False`, registry). The fixture emits a Sysmon `1 ProcessCreate`.
sim-t1562-004-atomic-disable-windows-fir.detection_logic: Alert on `netsh advfirewall set ... state off`, `Set-NetFirewallProfile -Enabled False`, and rule deletions/additions opening broad inbound access, especially on servers and outside change windows.
sim-t1562-004-atomic-disable-windows-fir.discriminators_tuning: Admins toggle firewall settings during troubleshooting — scope to "disable all profiles" and broad allow-rule creation, and correlate with change records.
sim-t1562-004-atomic-disable-windows-fir.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 1 (`netsh.exe`/PowerShell command lines), Microsoft-Windows-Windows Firewall/Operational `2003`/`2004` (settings changed), registry EID 13 on firewall policy keys.
sim-t1562-004-atomic-disable-windows-fir.title: Atomic event: Disable host firewall

sim-t1564-001-atomic-hidden-file-attribu.adversary_activity: Marking files hidden/system (`attrib +h +s`), using leading-dot names, or hiding data in Alternate Data Streams conceals payloads from casual inspection. The fixture emits a Sysmon `1 ProcessCreate`.
sim-t1564-001-atomic-hidden-file-attribu.detection_logic: Alert on `attrib +h +s` applied to files in user/temp/startup paths, on creation of ADS (`file.txt:hidden.exe`), and on hidden executables in user-writable directories. ADS execution is a strong evasion signal.
sim-t1564-001-atomic-hidden-file-attribu.discriminators_tuning: Installers set hidden/system attributes legitimately — weight on hidden *executables/scripts* in user paths and on ADS containing executable content.
sim-t1564-001-atomic-hidden-file-attribu.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 1 (`Image=attrib.exe`, `CommandLine`), EID 11/15 (file/ADS creation), EDR file telemetry.
sim-t1564-001-atomic-hidden-file-attribu.title: Atomic event: Hidden file attribute set

sim-t1566-001-atomic-email-attachment-de.adversary_activity: A targeted email delivers a weaponized attachment (macro-enabled Office doc, ISO/IMG, LNK, OneNote, password-protected archive) that executes on open. The fixture emits an email-gateway `MESSAGE_DELIVERED` event.
sim-t1566-001-atomic-email-attachment-de.detection_logic: Alert on delivered attachments of high-risk types (`.docm`, `.xlsm`, `.iso`, `.img`, `.lnk`, `.one`, `.hta`, double-extension, password-protected zip), on auth failures (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) from external senders impersonating internal domains, and on attachment hashes matching threat intel. The decisive follow-on signal is **Office/PDF reader spawning a script host or LOLBin** (chains into T1203/T1204.002).
sim-t1566-001-atomic-email-attachment-de.discriminators_tuning: Macro-enabled docs and archives are common in legitimate business mail — weight on sender reputation, auth results, and especially on post-delivery execution behavior rather than attachment type alone.
sim-t1566-001-atomic-email-attachment-de.production_log_sources: Email gateway/secure-email logs (sender, attachment name/type/hash, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, detonation verdict), O365/Exchange message trace, and — for execution follow-through — endpoint Sysmon EID 1/11 (Office spawning child processes, files written to Temp).
sim-t1566-001-atomic-email-attachment-de.title: Atomic event: Spearphishing attachment delivery

sim-t1567-002-atomic-exfil-to-cloud-stor.adversary_activity: Data is uploaded to consumer cloud storage (Dropbox, Google Drive, MEGA, OneDrive personal, pCloud) to blend with normal web traffic. The fixture emits a structured proxy `HTTP_UPLOAD`/`LargeUpload` event.
sim-t1567-002-atomic-exfil-to-cloud-stor.detection_logic: Alert on large `PUT`/`POST` uploads to consumer cloud-storage domains from hosts/users that don't normally use them, on upload-volume spikes to a single cloud destination, and on uploads to **personal** cloud accounts from corporate endpoints. Volume + unsanctioned-destination is the signal.
sim-t1567-002-atomic-exfil-to-cloud-stor.discriminators_tuning: Sanctioned cloud storage (corporate OneDrive/Drive) is normal — distinguish sanctioned tenants from personal accounts and baseline per-user upload volume; CASB tenant-awareness sharpens this greatly. --- # Impact
sim-t1567-002-atomic-exfil-to-cloud-stor.production_log_sources: Web proxy logs (destination domain, upload bytes, method), CASB/DLP, NDR/flow, DNS logs.
sim-t1567-002-atomic-exfil-to-cloud-stor.title: Atomic event: Exfiltration to cloud storage

sim-t1569-002-atomic-service-execution.adversary_activity: Tools like PsExec execute payloads by installing and starting a Windows service (PSEXESVC and clones), a hallmark of lateral movement and remote execution. The fixture emits a Windows System `7045 ServiceInstalled`.
sim-t1569-002-atomic-service-execution.detection_logic: Alert on `7045` with random/temporary service names, `ImagePath` pointing to `\Temp\`/`\ADMIN$\`/a randomly-named binary, `PSEXESVC` or known remote-exec service names, and on `services.exe` spawning `cmd.exe`/`powershell.exe`. Pair with a preceding `4624` network logon to attribute the source host.
sim-t1569-002-atomic-service-execution.discriminators_tuning: IT admins legitimately use PsExec and software installs create services — alert on suspicious binpaths and ephemeral service names rather than service creation broadly.
sim-t1569-002-atomic-service-execution.production_log_sources: Windows System log `7045` (ServiceInstalled: `ServiceName`, `ImagePath`), Security `4697` (service installed, audited), `7036`/`7040` (service state/start), Sysmon EID 1 (`ParentImage=services.exe`).
sim-t1569-002-atomic-service-execution.title: Atomic event: Service execution

sim-t1574-002-atomic-dll-side-loading.adversary_activity: A legitimate, signed executable is placed alongside a malicious DLL of a name it searches for; when run, the trusted process loads the attacker's DLL — executing malicious code under a trusted parent. The fixture emits a Sysmon `7 ImageLoaded`.
sim-t1574-002-atomic-dll-side-loading.detection_logic: Alert when a signed/trusted executable loads an **unsigned or invalidly-signed DLL** from the same user-writable directory, when a known-good system binary runs from a non-standard path with a co-located DLL, or when a DLL name matches a system DLL but loads from a user path.
sim-t1574-002-atomic-dll-side-loading.discriminators_tuning: Many legitimate apps load their own unsigned DLLs — baseline known image→DLL pairs and alert on trusted binaries loading DLLs from unexpected, user-writable locations.
sim-t1574-002-atomic-dll-side-loading.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoaded: `Image`, `ImageLoaded`, `Signed`, `SignatureStatus`), EID 1 (the host exe launching from an unusual path), EDR module-load telemetry.
sim-t1574-002-atomic-dll-side-loading.title: Atomic event: DLL side-loading artifact

sim-t1574-011-atomic-services-registry-p.adversary_activity: A weakly-ACL'd service registry key lets a low-privileged user rewrite the service's `ImagePath` to point at a malicious binary, which then runs at the service's (often SYSTEM) privilege. The fixture emits a Sysmon `13 RegistryValueSet`.
sim-t1574-011-atomic-services-registry-p.detection_logic: Alert on modification of a service's `ImagePath`/`FailureCommand`/`ServiceDll` to a user-writable path or LOLBin, especially when the modifying user is not an administrator. Pair with a subsequent service restart.
sim-t1574-011-atomic-services-registry-p.discriminators_tuning: Legitimate reconfiguration and patching change ImagePath — weight on non-admin modifiers and suspicious target paths.
sim-t1574-011-atomic-services-registry-p.production_log_sources: Sysmon EID 13 (RegistryValueSet on `HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\<svc>\ImagePath` or `FailureCommand`), Security `4657` (registry value modified), System `7040` (service config changed).
sim-t1574-011-atomic-services-registry-p.title: Atomic event: Service registry permissions weakness artifact

sim-t1589-web-user-enumeration.adversary_activity: The attacker probes login, registration, or password-reset endpoints to learn which usernames/emails exist, exploiting differences in response (message text, status code, or timing) between valid and invalid users. The harvested list feeds password spraying.
sim-t1589-web-user-enumeration.detection_logic: Alert on many distinct username probes from one source with few or zero password attempts per user, and on response-differential patterns (e.g., consistent 200-vs-404 or fast-vs-slow keyed to user existence). The catalog's `user-exists` flag is the key field.
sim-t1589-web-user-enumeration.discriminators_tuning: Self-service signup that checks username availability looks similar — scope to login/reset endpoints, or require a volume threshold and single-source correlation.
sim-t1589-web-user-enumeration.production_log_sources: Application auth logs (username, user-exists flag, outcome), web access logs, WAF logs.
sim-t1589-web-user-enumeration.title: Web user enumeration sequence

sim-t1595-http-fingerprint.adversary_activity: Before any attack, an actor fingerprints a public web/TLS endpoint — server banner, header order, TLS/JA3/JA4 signature, supported methods — to identify the platform and pick an exploit. This plan entry is a tabletop dry-run that only confirms authorization and starts the local telemetry server; it emits no attack traffic.
sim-t1595-http-fingerprint.detection_logic: Fingerprinting alone is low-signal; treat it as context, not an alert. Hunt for a single source touching many distinct endpoints with minimal request bodies, default tool TLS fingerprints, and HEAD/OPTIONS-heavy traffic. Correlate the source IP forward into later stages.
sim-t1595-http-fingerprint.discriminators_tuning: Search-engine crawlers, uptime/security monitors, and CDN health checks dominate the benign side. Maintain an allowlist of known scanners and your own monitoring infrastructure.
sim-t1595-http-fingerprint.production_log_sources: Web server access logs (NGINX/Apache/IIS), reverse-proxy/CDN/WAF logs, TLS-inspection or NDR metadata (JA3/JA4), netflow at the perimeter.
sim-t1595-http-fingerprint.title: HTTP/TLS service fingerprint plan

sim-t1595-http-method-probing.adversary_activity: Actors send uncommon HTTP verbs (`OPTIONS`, `TRACE`, `PUT`, `DELETE`, `CONNECT`) and method-override headers (`X-HTTP-Method-Override`) to map allowed methods, find write-enabled endpoints, or trigger TRACE-based XST.
sim-t1595-http-method-probing.detection_logic: Alert on `TRACE`/`TRACK` requests (almost never legitimate in modern apps), on `PUT`/`DELETE` to non-API paths, and on the presence of `X-HTTP-Method-Override` / `X-Method-Override`. A burst of `405 Method Not Allowed` from one source is a strong scanning signal.
sim-t1595-http-method-probing.discriminators_tuning: REST APIs legitimately use `PUT`/`DELETE`/`PATCH`; `OPTIONS` preflight is normal for CORS. Baseline allowed methods per route and alert on deviations rather than on the verbs globally.
sim-t1595-http-method-probing.production_log_sources: Web access logs (`method`, `status`), WAF logs, structured web JSON.
sim-t1595-http-method-probing.title: HTTP method and override probing

sim-t1595-tool-user-agent-fingerprint.adversary_activity: Reconnaissance and exploitation tools advertise themselves in the User-Agent header (`curl`, `python-requests`, `sqlmap`, `Nmap NSE`, `nikto`, `gobuster`). Seeing these against an app server usually means automated probing rather than a human browser.
sim-t1595-tool-user-agent-fingerprint.detection_logic: Alert on requests whose User-Agent matches a known offensive-tool or raw-HTTP-client pattern, especially `sqlmap`, `nikto`, `nmap`, `masscan`, `dirbuster`/`gobuster`, `feroxbuster`. Raise severity when paired with 4xx/5xx bursts or injection-shaped parameters.
sim-t1595-tool-user-agent-fingerprint.discriminators_tuning: `curl` and `python-requests` are heavily used by legitimate internal automation, API clients, and health checks — scope those to externally-facing hosts or require a second signal. UA strings are trivially spoofed, so absence of a tool UA does not mean absence of scanning.
sim-t1595-tool-user-agent-fingerprint.production_log_sources: Web access logs and structured web JSON (`user-agent` field), WAF logs.
sim-t1595-tool-user-agent-fingerprint.title: Scanner and tool user-agent fingerprinting

sim-t1595-web-404-burst.adversary_activity: Content/path discovery requests a list of common admin, CMS, and technology paths (`/admin`, `/wp-login.php`, `/phpmyadmin`, `/.git/`) looking for anything that returns 200. Most requests 404; the hits are the prize.
sim-t1595-web-404-burst.detection_logic: Alert when one source IP generates a high count of `404`s across distinct paths within a short window, especially against admin/CMS/known-tech paths. Critically, alert on any `200`/`301`/`403` *interleaved* in the 404 burst — that is a discovered resource.
sim-t1595-web-404-burst.discriminators_tuning: Broken links, misconfigured clients, and vulnerability scanners you run yourself produce 404 bursts. Tune the threshold to your traffic volume and exclude authorized scanner source IPs.
sim-t1595-web-404-burst.production_log_sources: Web access logs (`uri`, `status`, `client IP`, `user-agent`), WAF logs.
sim-t1595-web-404-burst.title: Web 404 discovery burst

sim-t1595-web-content-discovery.adversary_activity: Directory/file brute-forcing probes for admin consoles, API routes, backup files, and exposed source repositories to expand attack surface (`/api/v1/`, `/backup.zip`, `/.env`, `/.git/config`).
sim-t1595-web-content-discovery.detection_logic: Alert on a single source requesting many sensitive/uncommon paths in sequence, on dictionary-style path distributions, and on any successful retrieval of backup/config/repo paths. Combine path-diversity-per-source with a tool User-Agent for higher confidence.
sim-t1595-web-content-discovery.discriminators_tuning: Legitimate crawlers and link-checkers enumerate paths; scope to sensitive path lists and require either volume anomaly or a successful hit on a should-never-be-public resource.
sim-t1595-web-content-discovery.production_log_sources: Web access logs, WAF logs, structured web JSON (path distribution).
sim-t1595-web-content-discovery.title: Web content discovery and path enumeration
